LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MARCH-24-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL ENFORCEMENT OF ITS SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF RECORDS TO GALAXY SOLUTIONS INC.; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SUBPOENA SERVED BY DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY ON THIRD PARTY GALAXY SOLUTIONS, INC. IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE. GALAXY SOLUTIONS SHALL COMPLY WITH THE SUBPOENA IN FULL WITHIN 20 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SUBMITTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Enforcement Of Its Subpeona For Production Of Records To Galaxy Solutions Inc.
Off Calendar Mar-24-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Compel Enforcement Of Its Subpoena For Production Of Records To Galaxy Solutions Inc.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to enforce the subpoena served by defendant Pacific Gas & Electric Company on third party Galaxy Solutions, Inc. is granted. Defendant has shown good cause. Galaxy Solutions shall comply with the subpoena in full within 20 days of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Continued To Nov-13-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike is granted with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege facts showing malice, oppression or fraud. (Turman v. Turning Point of Central California, Inc. (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 53, 63-64 ["While the third amended complaint does allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action for gender discrimination, it fails to state facts sufficient to support allegations that respondent acted with malice, oppression or fraud, as required by statute. Appellant persists that the punitive damages allegations are adequately pled, relying primarily on the underlying facts associated with the cause of action for gender discrimination. However, as pled in this complaint, such facts do not rise to the level of malice, oppression or fraud necessary under Civ.Code, section 3294 to state a claim for punitive damages."].)

Order submitted.

Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RCE)
